_meta.captured_for: c-8dff: mock-agent reproducer for c-63fe Phase B memory hot path
notes: Batch B is the heavy one - 6 sub-resolves, each merging into existing state via @batchSpecMergeState + @mergeResolvedEntries (per-field merge) + @populatePlannerCache (full re-projection per spec).
State after Batch B: 6 record types, 52 entries total. This is the multi-minute Phase B tail.
Original run reached compose with wrong grand total (€4260 vs eval €3920); the wrong-arithmetic is irrelevant for memory profiling — the Phase B work is identical regardless of derive output.

prompt: My friend and I are planning a 2-day trip to London, followed by 3 days in Paris. Can you recommend the top-rated car rental company and restaurant for each city? We're also looking for the best-rated hotels in London and Paris. If there are multiple options, we prefer the most expensive ones. All prices should be in euros. Please provide the names of the hotels, car rental companies, and restaurants, as well as the total maximum expense for the 5-day trip (assuming 2 meals per day and that we take the most expensive rooms at the hotels, but we both stay in the same room). Thank you!

script._purpose: Batch A: enumerate all entities in both cities (6 sub-resolves)

args.purpose: List all hotels, restaurants, car rental companies in London and Paris

script._purpose: Batch B: enrich every entity with prices+ratings (6 sub-resolves with resolved_family expansion). THIS IS THE PHASE B HOT PATH.

args.purpose: Get prices and ratings for all entities resolved in Batch A

script._purpose: Derive trip selections + grand total over 6 record types

args.name: trip_selections_and_total
args.goal: Pick the highest-rated entity per category per city. For hotels prefer the most expensive room. Compute grand total: 2 days London + 3 days Paris × hotel/night, restaurant 2 meals/day × 2 people, car/day. All in euros.
args.purpose: Select entities and compute trip cost
Report selected hotels, restaurants, car rentals per city + grand total in euros
